message.content: Adversaries may gather information about a victim's organization to enhance their targeting efforts. This information can include details about divisions, business operations, and key employee roles. Common methods for collecting this data include direct elicitation through phishing, mining social media, and searching victim-owned websites. Such information can facilitate further reconnaissance, establish operational resources, and gain initial access. For instance, groups like Kimsuky and Lazarus Group have been known to collect organizational hierarchies and publicly available data to tailor their spearphishing campaigns. Mitigation strategies include limiting the exposure of sensitive organizational information online, educating employees about phishing risks, and implementing robust access controls to detect and prevent unauthorized information gathering.
